title: Drive synthesis from Claude subscription, never the pay-per-token API
context: Claude Code auth precedence puts ANTHROPIC_API_KEY above subscription OAuth and always uses it in headless `-p` mode, so users with an API key silently billed the pay-per-token API instead of their subscription whenever synthesis ran.
decision: Removed AnthropicProvider entirely; ClaudeCliProvider now strips ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from its spawned env to force subscription auth (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / `/login`). Synthesis runs from tmpdir (neutral cwd, no tools, --max-turns 1) to avoid loading the repo's brain MCP server. Updated doctor to surface auth path and remediation.
consequences: Synthesis requires Claude subscription or CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN; users with only an API key must set up subscription auth
API credentials in env are now silently ignored for synthesis (prevents accidental billing to API)
Synthesis runs in isolation, cheaper and no risk of recursive brain loads
Haiku alias (not pinned dated ID) survives model retirements
Doctor output now explicitly states which auth path is active and how to fix it
alternatives_rejected: Document the precedence and let users manage it (silent billing remains a trap)
Make API vs subscription user-selectable (adds config complexity; subscription should be default only)
Keep AnthropicProvider but make it opt-in (still risky if enabled by accident)
Conditionally strip the key (blanket strip is simpler and forces the right path always)
